title: Count Leading Zeroes in Binary Representation

statement.markdown:
Given a non-negative integer n, count the number of leading zeroes in its binary representation.

You can use the built-in methods of the data types.
statement.outputFormat: The count of leading zeroes in the binary representation of n.

examples.explanation: Binary representation of 10 is 1010, so the count of leading zeroes is 2.

hints.text: Recall that to count the number of leading zeroes in a binary number, you can use the built-in methods of the data types.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: You can use the built-in int function to convert the integer to a binary string, and then count the number of leading zeroes.
editorial.bruteForce: You can convert the integer to a binary string and then count the number of leading zeroes.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the built-in int function to convert the integer to a binary string and count the number of leading zeroes.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the built-in int function to convert the integer to a binary string and count the number of leading zeroes.
